description_long: This church features a tall, steeply pitched roof with dark brown shingles and stone masonry walls, giving it a robust and historic appearance. The structure includes a prominent square bell tower with arched openings and a cross at the apex, suggesting its religious function. The main body of the church is rectangular, with a smaller, semi-cylindrical apse at the rear and several small, arched windows and doors along the sides. The roof is multi-tiered, with the central nave higher than the side aisles, and the stonework is accented by visible buttresses and cornerstones. The interior, partially visible in one view, reveals wooden roof trusses and a simple, open nave. The overall design is reminiscent of Romanesque architecture, with its solid construction, rounded arches, and minimal ornamentation.
description: A stone church with a steep, dark shingled roof, a square bell tower topped by a cross, and a semi-cylindrical apse at the rear, featuring Romanesque architectural elements.
description_view_0: Side view showing the long stone wall, two arched doors, and the tall bell tower with a steep roof.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the semi-cylindrical apse, layered rooflines, and the cross atop the tower.
description_view_2: Cutaway side view revealing the wooden roof trusses and open nave inside the church.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the main entrance at the base of the bell tower and the cross above.
